verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: Athanasius could not write to one who did not even know him.

verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: For myself, I am so surprised at the enormity of the thing, that I am quite uncertain what I ought to say in my defence.

verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: I can only declare, that I condemn myself to die ten thousand deaths, if even the least suspicion attaches to me in this matter.

verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: And to you, Sire, as a lover of the truth, I confidently make my appeal.

verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: I beseech you, as I said before, investigate this affair, and especially with the testimony of those who were once sent by him as ambassadors to you.

verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: These are the Bishops Sarvatius and Maximus and the rest, with Clementius and Valens.

verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: Enquire of them, I beseech you, whether they brought letters to me.

verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: If they did, this would give me occasion to write to him.

verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: But if he did not write to me, if he did not even know me, how could I write to one with whom I had no acquaintance?

verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: Ask them whether, when I saw Clementius and his fellows, and spoke of your brother of blessed memory, I did not, in the language of Scripture, wet my garments with tears, when I remembered his kindness of disposition and his Christian spirit.

verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: Learn of them how anxious I was, on hearing of the cruelty of the beast, and finding that Valens and his company had come by way of Libya, lest he should attempt a passage also, and like a robber murder those who held in love and memory the departed Prince, among whom I account myself second to none.
